# Ingredients

Grocery List

 \normalsize

- 1 cup spinach

- 2 clementines

- 1.5 cups of some combination of blueberry, blueberry, blackberry, and strawberries
- 1 tsp of honey
- 2 green onions
- 1 large poblano
- 2 roma tomatoes
- 1 can of black beans
- 1 tablespoon southwest spice
- 6-8 flour tortillas
- 1 jar of salsa verde
- 4 oz monterey jack cheese

- 4 brioche buns

- 1 red onion
- shredded lettuce

\newpage

# Recipe One

 \normalsize

![Enchiladas](website/images/Enchiladas.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 2 green onions
- 1 large poblano
- 2 roma tomatoes
- 1 can of black beans
- 1 tablespoon southwest spice
- 6-8 flour tortillas
- 1 jar of salsa verde
- 4 oz monterey jack cheese
- 2 tablespoons sour cream

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Dice tomato and poblano. Thinly slice green onions, separating greens and whites. Drain (save liquid) and rinse black beans.
1. Cook poblano in oil over medium heat until softened (5-7 minutes).
1. Add almost all of the southwest seasoning (except a half tsp), tomato, green onion whites, half the black beans, and 1/8 cup black bean liquid. Cook another 5-10 minutes, until peppers are tender.
1. Add remanining beans and a drizzle of oil in a small sauce pan over medium heat.
1. Add 1/4 cup black bean liquid and bring to a simmer. 6. Add 1 tablespoon of butter and cook until melted.
1. Using a silicone masher, the black beans and butter until smooth.
1. Spread black bean mixture over tortillas and evenly split filling between tortillas. Roll up tortillas and place them in a 9 x 13 baking dish.
1. Top with salsa verde and monterey jack cheese. Bake at 450 for 5-10 minutes until cheese melts and starts to brown.
1. Combine sour cream and 1/2 tsp southwest seasoning. Mix in 1 teaspoon of water at a time until sauce can be drizzled. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
1. Serve enchiladas with sauce and green onion greens.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Two

 \normalsize

![Burgers with pasta salad](website/images/Burgers.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1 pound 80/20 ground beef
- 4 brioche buns
- 4 slices of cheddar cheese
- pickle chips
- 1 tomato
- 1 red onion
- shredded lettuce
- 1 pound pasta (rigatoni or penne)
- 1 cup mayonnaise
- 1 can black olives
- 1 white onion

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Boil pasta according to package instructions. Place in a large bowl and cool to room temperature.
1. Dice onion and drain olives.
1. Combine pasta, onion, olives and mayonnaise. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
1. Refrigerate over night.
1. Thinly slice the tomato and red onion.
1. Mix ground beef with salt and pepper.
1. Create 4 evenly-sized patties. Put a dimple in the middle so it cooks to a consistent thickness.
1. Lightly toast the bun.
1. Build the burger by layering the burger, sliced tomato, onion, and pickles. Add ketchup, mustard, and mayonnaise to taste.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Three


 \normalsize

![Meatloaf and Baked Carrots](website/images/Meatloaf.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 6 garlic cloves
- 2 teaspoon ginger paste
- 1 shallot
- 16 oz turkey
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup panko breadcrumbs
- 4 medium-to-large carrots
- 1/4 cup General Tso’s or Hoisin Sauce

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Preheat oven to 450 degrees. Chop shallot and mince garlic.
1. In a small bowl, mix carrots, 2 tablespoons olive oil, and salt and pepper to taste.
1. In a medium bowl, mix turkey, ginger, two-thirds of the garlic, shallot, egg, panko breadcrumbs, a teaspoon of salt, and a pinch of pepper.
1. Form four mini meatloafs using the turkey mixture and generously brush with sauce.
1. Bake meatloaf and carrots on a rimmed baking sheet for 20 minutes. Sprinkle remaining garlic on carrots and let sit for 2-3 minutes.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Breakfast Recipe

 \normalsize

![Berry Melon Smoothie](website/images/BerryMelonSmoothie.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1/2 cup cranberry juice
- 1 cup spinach
- 1/2 tsp ginger paste
- 2 clementines
- 1/3 cup watermelon
- 1.5 cups of some combination of blueberry, blueberry, blackberry, and strawberries
- 1 tsp of honey

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. In a blender, combine 1/2 cup of water, cranberry juice, spinach, ginger, and clementines until smooth.
1. Add watermelon and berries, and blend until smooth.
1. Serve in mason jars!

 \normalsize
